name: Sarutahiko
description: Sarutahiko Ōkami is a prominent deity in Japanese Shinto mythology, often depicted as a towering figure with a large nose, ruddy face, and long beard. He is revered as the leader of the earthly kami, particularly those associated with crossroads and boundaries. His role is primarily that of a guide and protector, notably facilitating the descent of Ninigi-no-Mikoto, the grandson of Amaterasu, to earth. Sarutahiko's imposing appearance underscores his strength and authority, while his guidance highlights his wisdom and benevolence. He is considered a deity of safe passage, agriculture, and martial arts, embodying the power and stability necessary for societal order and prosperity. He is often associated with the Kunitsu-kami, the earthly deities who predate the arrival of the Amatsukami, the heavenly deities.
subtitle: Leader of the Earthly Kami

keyMyths.title: The Descent of Ninigi
keyMyths.description: Sarutahiko plays a crucial role in the myth of Ninigi-no-Mikoto's descent from Takamagahara (the High Celestial Plain) to Ashihara no Nakatsukuni (the Central Land of Reed Plains). Amaterasu, the sun goddess, sends her grandson Ninigi to rule the earth. Sarutahiko, with his imposing figure, initially intimidates the heavenly delegation, but Ame-no-Uzume, the goddess of dance, uses her wit and charm to appease him. Sarutahiko then guides Ninigi and his entourage safely to their destination, thus ensuring the divine lineage's establishment on earth.

keyMyths.title: Ame-no-Uzume and Sarutahiko
keyMyths.description: Ame-no-Uzume's encounter with Sarutahiko is a pivotal moment in Japanese mythology. As the heavenly deities descend, Sarutahiko stands in their way. Ame-no-Uzume, known for her boldness and captivating dance, confronts him. She uses her disrobing dance to both entertain and intimidate Sarutahiko, ultimately persuading him to yield and guide the heavenly delegation. This encounter highlights the power of diplomacy and the importance of balance between strength and cunning.

keyMyths.title: Sarutahiko's Death
keyMyths.description: Sarutahiko's death is described in some accounts as occurring while he was fishing in the sea. A giant clam pinched his hand, and he subsequently drowned. This seemingly anticlimactic end for such a powerful deity underscores the unpredictable nature of life and the inevitability of death, even for gods. It also highlights the connection between Sarutahiko and the natural world, emphasizing his role as a deity deeply intertwined with the earth and its elements.
